title: Hattie and the Celestial Shooting Star
summary: Hattie the Mouse embarks on a celestial adventure after meeting a shooting star named Vijaya and a sparkly bird named Badasta. Together, they soar across the night sky, experiencing the thrill of flight before landing safely back at Hattie’s cozy tree house. Exhausted from their journey, the trio skips their snack and settles in for a peaceful nap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: [Settling noises]

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'll get my nighties.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What are they?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nighty-thrashes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. All right. Turn out the light. And now, it's time for Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here's a star.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Shall we tell a story about a star?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Hattie. Always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The story of Hattie the Mouse who wanted to meet a star.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie, here's a star.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Isabella, I didn't know you had one. Can you show me?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's a star, and if you open it like this, it's a clip. It's a shooting star.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. It's a star and a clip and a shooting star. Can you show me how it shoots across the sky?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: [Shooting sound]

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, look at it go. Hello shooting star, what's your name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Vijaya.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Vijaya?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I have a bird friend. [Chirping sounds]

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh wow, says Hattie. Nice to meet you bird friend. What's your name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Badasta.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Badasta? Oh, hi Badasta. And what color was Badasta?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She's sparkly Badasta.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She's sparkly. Oh, a sparkly bird. And Hattie said wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh no, where's my clip?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Did it fall down?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Here it is.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here it is.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now Hattie was very excited to meet the shooting star and the sparkly bird. And she decided to see if they wanted to go on an adventure together. And she said, "Excuse me, Vijaya and Badasta, can we go on an adventure together?" And what did they say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, yes!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Should we go shooting across the sky like shooting stars or should we go to the pond and have a swim or somewhere else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know, but I have a song for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You do? What's the song?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Shooting the star and then you'll find out!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Was that the song?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie and her two new friends decided to go shooting across the sky. And they asked the shooting star to show them how.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, just like this, you shoot across the sky. Zoom!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie the Mouse was just a little mouse. She was used to living under the ground and in her little hollow tree workshop. And shooting across the sky was a big new experience for her and she was excited and a little nervous, but she followed the shooting star and shot across the sky. Zoom!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zoom! Shoo! You shoot and then you land down like that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and then they all landed together. Where did they land?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They landed on top... they landed inside of Hattie's tree house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They came all the way back and landed in Hattie's tree house. And then Hattie said, "Wow, that was exciting. I think after an adventure like that I need a little snack. What do you like to eat, shooting star?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: [Snoring sounds]

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're already asleep. Oh, how sweet. And so Hattie and the sparkly bird and the shooting star decided they were too tired to have a snack and they took a nap. The end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think this is the end of Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Vijaya
characters.description: A shooting star that can transform into a hair clip and lead adventures across the sky.

characters.name: Badasta
characters.description: A sparkly bird friend who joins Hattie on her nighttime journey.

places.name: Hollow Tree Workshop
places.description: Hattie's home and workplace located under the ground.

places.name: The Sky
places.description: The expansive setting where the characters shoot across the stars.

places.name: The Pond
places.description: A secluded pond nestled at the field’s edge, its still waters beckon as a perfect swimming hole for weary adventurers, while the silty shallows yield a curious bounty of smooth, sun-bleached shells.

places.name: Hattie's Tree House
places.description: The cozy destination where the friends land and eventually take a nap.
